resources.string: Kirsch needs camera access to scan photos
Fill the frame with the photo,\nthen tap the shutter
Move the phone in slow circles\nuntil the ring is complete
No scans yet — scan your first photo
Capture ended with status %1$s; the acquisition is retained but not processed
RAW acquisition saved. Processing is disabled until the DNG color pipeline is validated.

The glare-removal sweep is the default. It keeps capturing while you move the phone and finishes once the views differ enough to remove reflections. The other modes exist for benchmarking and diagnostics.
Recorded in capture packages so benchmark scans can be traced to a physical print. Leave empty outside benchmark sessions.
Zips acquisition and scan packages to a location you choose, for offline analysis. Scans export together with their source captures.
Shows which processing features are available on this build, with recorded reasons for anything unavailable.
Kirsch has no network permission. Photos, captures, and scans stay on this device unless you export them.
No completed acquisition or scan packages to export
%1$s — adjust the corners if needed, then save
Single-frame scan ready (fusion was not possible)
Drag a handle to its print corner — a magnifier shows the exact spot while you drag.
Applies the corner correction and updates the scan. You can re-adjust and apply again.
Manual print corner editor with four draggable handles
Each enhancement is saved as a new copy — the original scan is always kept.
Adds the finished scan to your photo library (Pictures/Kirsch) and locks this version. Sources stay on the device for reprocessing.
Saved to your photo library (Pictures/Kirsch). This scan is now locked; its sources remain on the device.
Optional: record the print’s physical size so the scan carries true PPI metadata.
Sampling frequency recorded: %1$.1f × %2$.1f PPI. No delivered-resolution claim was made.
